failure_modes.text: Teams can keep defending existing tradition by selectively seeking confirming evidence that the balance point should not move.
failure_modes.source_quote: tradition-vs-innovation-balance conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when teams seek evidence that confirms the existing tradition while dismissing signals that the balance point needs to shift.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the evidence base for the status quo: require defenders of tradition to show current evidence that the inherited approach still outperforms alternatives, not just that it has worked before.

failure_modes.text: Innovation can degrade into theater when novelty is rewarded as identity instead of as evidence-backed adaptation.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when innovation becomes an identity performance
failure_modes.mitigation: Require innovation to earn its place with evidence: treat novelty the same as tradition — it must demonstrate a testable advantage, not just signal identity or ambition.

failure_modes.text: Tradition can turn into sunk-cost protection or fear instead of preserving something that still works.
failure_modes.source_quote: tradition becomes cover for fear, sunk-cost protection, or avoidance of evidence
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish preservation from fear: when tradition is defended, check whether the real motive is that the old way still works or that the switching cost feels too threatening.

premortem_questions.text: Which element actually needs to remain standardized, and which one are we only protecting because changing it feels risky?
premortem_questions.source_quote: explicitly separate which elements should remain standardized from which elements should be experimented on

premortem_questions.text: What evidence would convince us that the current balance point has stopped matching reality?
premortem_questions.source_quote: dismissing signals that the balance point needs to shift

premortem_questions.text: Are we treating novelty as proof of improvement or only as a signal to test?

heuristics.text: Separate stable components from experimental components before debating the pace of change.
heuristics.source_quote: explicitly separate which elements should remain standardized from which elements should be experimented on

heuristics.text: Use tradition to preserve proven reliability, not to shield weak assumptions from evidence.
heuristics.source_quote: preserve reliability, safety, or institutional memory

heuristics.text: Demand execution discipline from innovation proposals so novelty does not become its own credential.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on explicit separation between what should stay stable and what should be tested rather than on generic change-management language.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
